observations: tests/bench_optimization_final.py correctly mirrors bench_optimization_baseline.py structure with all new optimized kernels
bench/optimization_final.json saved with 12 kernel category results matching expected format
bench/optimization_report.md contains comprehensive side-by-side comparison table with computed speedup ratios
All 4 non-regression test files pass: test_flash_attn_256.py, test_gemm_fp16_prefill.py, test_elementwise.py, test_gemv_int4.py
All 7 new .hip kernel files compile successfully for gfx906 (exit code 0 for each)
5 of 6 optimization areas show measurable improvement (FA prefill 1.58-1.99x, INT4 GEMM 1.4-2.07x, FP16 GEMM 1.02-1.09x, GEMV 1.04-1.12x, elementwise 1.01-1.33x, fused_skip_rmsnorm_gemv 1.07x)
Report correctly identifies the SiLU epilogue GEMM regression at M=128 and provides correct analysis (compute-bound regime, fusion overhead > savings at small batch)

validationContractCoverage.VAL-FINAL-001: pass - bench/optimization_final.json created with all kernel timings from bench_optimization_final.py run
validationContractCoverage.VAL-FINAL-002: pass - bench/optimization_report.md shows side-by-side comparison, 5 of 6 areas improved (exceeds threshold of 3)
validationContractCoverage.VAL-CROSS-001: pass - all 4 existing test files pass with no regressions
validationContractCoverage.VAL-CROSS-002: pass - all 7 new .hip files compile for gfx906 with exit code 0

codeReviewNotes: bench_optimization_final.py uses proper error handling with try_build_kernel/get_kernel_safe wrappers - graceful skip on any build failure
Benchmark methodology is consistent with baseline: same WARMUP_ITERS=10, BENCH_ITERS=100, statistics.median() for latency
GPU memory is properly freed after each kernel section (dev.free calls)
pack_w4a16 helper function correctly packs INT4 weights for the v4 GEMV format
Grid calculations are consistent with kernel configurations (e.g., cols_per_wg = 256 // tpc for GEMV)
The optimization_report.md contains thorough analysis including performance regime analysis (bandwidth vs compute bound)
Minor: bench script uses warnings.warn for skip messages rather than logging - acceptable for benchmark scripts
No sensitive data or credentials are present in any of the committed files

sharedStateObservations.description: Final benchmark results confirm best configurations: INT4 GEMV decode should use v4_t8 (best balance: 116 GB/s at N=4096) or v4_t4 for very small N; t16 shows slight regression vs v3 at N=4096 due to fdot2 dequantization overhead

Kernel fusion analysis: fused_skip_rmsnorm_gemv provides 7% speedup (139 us vs 149 us estimated separate); gemm_fp16_prefill_silu_epilogue regresses at M=128 (compute-bound); fusion only helps for bandwidth-bound operations
